ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF TRANS PACIFIC NATIONAL BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF TRANS PACIFIC NATIONAL BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANTS RAYMOND M. BUDDIE AND KAIA BALSZ IS GRANTED. THE BANK SATISFIED ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION BY PRESENTING EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT EACH ELEMENT OF ITS CLAIMS. (SEE CCP 437C(P)(1).) TRANS PACIFIC NATIONAL BANK IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT IN ITS FAVOR IN THE AMOUNT OF $454,307.63 PLUS DAILY INTEREST AT THE RATE OF $58.78 FROM NOVEMBER 2, 2016 TO THE DATE OF JUDGMENT AGAINST MR. BUDDIE AND MS. BALSZ. WRITTEN ORDER AND JUDGMENT ARE SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Off Calendar Mar-16-2017 Text Ruling

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF TRANS PACIFIC NATIONAL BANK's Motion For Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Statutory Costs Pursuant To Judgment is granted. MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-25-2017 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-25-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Michael C. Starkey of the law offices of French Lyon Tang (415.597.7816) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Matthew P. Pachkowski of the law offices of Steiner & Pachkowski (650.559.8100) appears as counsel for defendant/non-opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Trans Pacific National Bank's motion for summary judgment against defendants Raymond M. Buddie and Kaia Balsz is GRANTED. The Bank satisfied its initial burden of production by presenting evidence to support each element of its claims. (See CCP 437c(p)(1).) Plaintiff is not obligated to disprove the defendants' affirmative defenses to meet its summary judgment burden. Ms. Balsz did not oppose the motion. Mr. Buddie filed an opposing points and authorities. Mr. Buddie filed no other documents that create a triable issue of material fact, however. There is no opposing evidence and Mr. Buddie's failure to provide an opposing separate statement is grounds to grant the motion by itself. (CCP 437c(b)(3).) Mr. Buddie refers to outstanding discovery, but does not provide a declaration in compliance with CCP 437c(h) to warrant a continuance of the motion. In addition, Mr. Buddie fails to explain his diligence in conducting discovery. "A good faith showing that further discovery is needed to oppose summary judgment requires some justification for why such discovery could not have been completed sooner...[the] lack of diligence may be a factor in justifying the refusal to grant a continuance under section 437c, subdivision (h)." (Cooksey v. Alexakis, (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 246, 257.) Accordingly, the motion is granted. Trans Pacific National Bank is entitled to judgment in its favor in the amount of $454,307.63 plus daily interest at the rate of $58.78 from November 2, 2016 to the date of judgment against Mr. Buddie and Ms. Balsz. Written order and judgment are signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (D302)
